fields.description:
## What

Generate `docs/mcp-reference.md` from the MCP server's OWN registrars, correctly
classified by gate, and gate it in CI. Mirrors the `gen_env_registry.py` generator +
drift-gate pattern.

## Source of truth (verified — registration-time vs call-time gating)
The server (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py` `build_server()`) assembles THREE registrars:
- `register_read_tools` (`_mcp_reads.py`) — 23 tools, ALWAYS registered.
- `register_llm_tools` (`_mcp_llm.py`) — 6 tools, ALWAYS registered; gated at CALL time on
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`.
- `register_write_tools` (`_mcp_writes.py`) — 18 tools, CONDITIONALLY registered: returns
  early when `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` is truthy, so they are ABSENT from `list_tools()` in
  read-only mode. Totals verified: 47 tools (readonly off) / 29 (readonly on).

**Critical:** only `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` changes list membership. `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` and
`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` are call-time only (tools always listed, refuse at call). So a
naive single `list_tools()` in ambient env silently produces a PARTIAL reference. The
generator MUST enumerate each registrar with `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0` so writes register.

## Classification rule (deterministic — registrar default + closed 3-entry special-case table)
There is NO per-tool gate attribute, and `readOnlyHint`/`destructiveHint` do NOT map to the
env gate. Classify by which registrar registers the tool, then apply the closed override table:
| Group (registrar) | Default gate | Special-case overrides |
|---|---|---|
| READS (23) | none (always available) | `reconcile` -> `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` (live mode; the sole jira-gate consumer); `fsck` -> `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` (recover path only) |
| LLM (6) | `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` (call-time) | `sign_review` -> `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` (HYBRID: in the LLM registrar but write-gated, makes no LLM call) |
| WRITES (18) | `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` (registration-time; absent when readonly) | — |
`sign_manifest` is a WRITE-registrar tool (not the LLM `sign_review` — do not conflate).
Gate descriptions come from the `MCP_ENV_VARS` constant (`mcp_server.py`), the same source
`rebar-mcp --help` uses, so the doc cannot drift from `--help`.

## Scope
- Add `scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py` (new): enumerate each registrar onto its own FastMCP
  with `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0` (writes register); read each tool's name + first-line summary
  (from `.description`); classify via the table above; emit `docs/mcp-reference.md` with a
  generated header and FOUR clearly-labeled sections: **Read-only (always)**,
  **LLM-gated (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`)**, **Write-gated (`REBAR_MCP_READONLY`)**, and the
  jira-gated `reconcile` live mode — with the three special cases (`reconcile`, `fsck`,
  `sign_review`) explicitly annotated. Include the `MCP_ENV_VARS` gate descriptions.
- `--check` drift mode + CI gate in `.github/workflows/test.yml` (mirror env-registry).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/mcp-reference.md` is generated (marked generated) from the live registrars and
      lists all 47 tools (23 read + 6 llm + 18 write).
- [ ] Tools are structurally separated into labeled sections by gate tier (NOT prose) — a
      Read-only section, an LLM-gated section, and a Write-gated section each exist (verify,
      prints nothing):
      `for s in 'Read-only' 'LLM-gated' 'Write-gated'; do grep -qi "$s" docs/mcp-reference.md || echo "NO SECTION $s"; done`
- [ ] The three special cases are correctly placed/annotated: `sign_review` appears in the
      write-gated (`REBAR_MCP_READONLY`) tier NOT the llm-gated tier; `reconcile` is annotated
      as jira-gated (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`); `fsck` recover is readonly-gated. (Verify by
      reading the generated sections.)
- [ ] Count proof (deterministic, prints nothing):
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py && [ "$(grep -cE '^\| ?`?[a-z_]+`? ' docs/mcp-reference.md)" -ge 47 ] || echo "TOO FEW TOOLS"` (or an equivalent explicit count of listed tool rows >= 47).
- [ ] Drift gate works — adding/removing a tool changes output; a stale committed file fails
      `--check` (deterministic, prints `CAUGHT`):
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py; printf '\nstale\n' >> docs/mcp-reference.md; python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py --check >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MISS || echo CAUGHT; python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py`
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` stay green.

Files: `scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py` (new), `docs/mcp-reference.md` (new/generated),
`.github/workflows/test.yml`.
